entry.title: Re: #SAPHANA Query Hints - Looking for documentation
entry.content: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/4ba9edce1f2347a0b9fcda99879c17a1.html

entry.title: Re: Block Planning tab has no details for characteristics...
entry.content:
Only activities with type Produce are relevant for block planning.

I think the activity that you have used belongs to Setup or something (other than Produce).

entry.title: How to add Group Rule field User -> User Group table in Story Schema
entry.content: Hi. Is it possible to add Users Group Rule field to User Group table in Story Report? In ORD report this field is reportable along with Users Group ID and Users Group Name in User Users Group table and returns XML code of the report definition. Thank you.

entry.title: Download CTMS transport logs by using Cloud Foundry CL
entry.content:
1. Background

little by little, the manual export/import has been replaced by the SAP BTP Cloud Transport Management Service(CTMS) for transporting any SAP Integraiton Suite(IS) Contents, including any Cloud Integration(CI) content and API management content.

When bellow error occurrs, how should we do to find out the cause? 

 The most straightforward way is to view the 'Transport Action Log' as below

obviously the information is not enough for you to determine the cause. You may notice the highlighted statement for details inform. In the below content, I will guide you reach there and see what kind of information those deployment log will provide and whether they are helpful or not.

1.1 Include

  • CF8 Installation
  • MultiApps CLI Plugin Installation
  • CF8 CL operation

1.2 Exclude

  • SAP CI Development
  • API Management Configuration
  • SAP BTP CTMS Configuration

1.3 Prerequsite

  • Multiple Tier(at least two) SAP Integration Suite Subaccount
  • CTMS has been configured between/among above subaccounts

1.4 Targer Reader

  • Integration Developer
  • Basis

Here are the detail steps:

2. Install CF CLI

This is the official link provided in the CTMS Log:

https://docs.cloudfoundry.org/cf-cli/install-go-cli.html

however i cannot find the one for windows platform. So if you are using Windows, please go to here:

https://github.com/cloudfoundry/cli/wiki/V8-CLI-Installation-Guide

Please ignore the 'Chocolatey' part. I have tried and find it tedious. You can download the installer file directly via here:

no need the admin access, you can install it and after installation, the folder looks like this:

3. Install MultiApps(MTA) CLI Plugin

This is the link provided in the CTMS Log for your reference:

https://github.com/cloudfoundry/multiapps-cli-plugin

My preference is this link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/install-multiapps-cli-plugin-in-cloud-foundry-environment

anyway the key part is here:

Please be aware that, in the example, the command cf has been used. whereas in our case, the CF8 is installed, therefore, cf8 is the relevant command for us. I won't explain it later. 

Launch the windows powershell, and go to the folder where cf8 has been installed so that you can execute it.

install the MTA plugin by executing below command:

 

 

cf8 add-plugin-repo CF-Community https://plugins.cloudfoundry.org

 

 

then

 

 

cf8 install-plugin multiapps -f

 

 

This is how it looks like on my machine. It might a little difference on yours. because the MTA addon has already installed on my computer.

4. Get into BTP Global Account via CF CLI

Find out the API Endpoint of the Cloud Foundry Space of the TARGET subaccount.

In my case, I have two BTP IS subaccounts. Say BTP IS Dev and BTP IS QA. Then go to the subaccount of BTP IS QA via web browser.

If you do not know how to find it, here is a reference of mine.

Now switch back to the windows powershell.

4.1 Specify the endpoint

This is the command format

 

 

cf8 api <your CF endpoint>

 

 

 This is an example of mine

The highlighted login command will be explained next.

4.2 Login Options

There are two ways to login, which has been highlighted in the above step.

by using cf8.exe login or simply cf8 login, you will login by using the default identity provider. 

If you have multiple identity provider, normally the Microsoft Active Directory, and you want to login by using any user under custom identity provider, please use cf8.exe login --sso or simply cf8 login --sso.

By this means, you will have the option to chose the identity provider and retrieve the login token.

For my case, I just login by using the default identity provider. So simply cf8 login

as you can see above, for me, there are four cloud foundry orgnizations under different subaccounts have been listed for me. For you, the list of orgs will be different.

please choose the ITEM NUMBER of the TARGET subaccount. 

So for my case, the target subaccount is 'integration-qa', so i should input '4' here. Please be aware that, DO NOT input item number of CTMS.

This is the operation for me.

5. Download Logs

Once you get into the subaccount or org, you can simple replicate the command from the CTMS Log and execute it here. Let's take the one in chapter 1 as an example

Now the relevant deployment log has been downloaded into your CF CLI folder as below. The folder name should be relevant to the deployment id used.

6. Analyse the log

The log folder created normally contains files like this

if the TR does contain multiple transported contents, there will be multiple log files with GUID format names in the folder.

This is an example of the content of the file whose name is with GUID format

This is an example of the content of the file, CTS_LOG

File OPERATION.log has most content. I just get part of them, which contains java exception captured.

Apparently these contents do not quite make sense to me as an Integration developer unfortunitely :-(. Not sure if they ring any bells to anyone who has basis background or to SAP.

7. My personal recommendation for BTP IS Transportation.

Here are few my experience when using CTMS v1.90.1 for transporting SAP BTP Integration Suite contents.

7.1 for Cloud Integration Content.

If the transport failed, you can manually delete the one in the target subaccount and import it again in CTMS.

7.2 For API Proxy of API Management

If the transprot failed, please try to manually export/import. If you encountered any error in the manul mode, it cannot be transported via CTMS. 

7.3 For product of API Management

My feeling is that, when a product has been transported in a TR, all of its API Proxy(s) will be transported at the same time. However when an API proxy is transported, the configuration in its product won't be transported. Therefore any transportation failure of any belonged API proxy will impact the transportation of product. 

If the transport of product failed, please check if all its API proxies are working fine and can be transported or not. 

By the way, I would recommend manully configure the product rather than transport it. Think about one of the API proxy is still under constructure by someone else and you transport the product. The TR will import the un-tested API proxy to the target which is not what you want. so, 

Transport on the API proxy level and manually add it to the product in the subsequential system 🙂 This is my personal proposal based on the current version of CTMS.

Conclusion

The downloaded deployment log looks not quite helpful. Further analysis is required on it. However it is a way to provide detail evidence to SAP for solving the transportation issue in CTMS.

entry.title: Custom Learning LxP Integration with SAP SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP SuccessFactors LMS offers Learning Experience Platform (LxP) integration via the Open Content Network (OCN). However, some clients may already be using a third-party LxP that they wish to integrate with SuccessFactors LMS. This document outlines the steps for such integration. Before proceeding, it is recommended to encourage clients to leverage the Open Content Network to simplify integration. If the client is not willing to transition, the following steps will guide the integration process.

Prerequisites:

  1. The LxP should support Single Sign-On (SSO).
  2. The LxP must be capable of sending data via API or flat file (SFTP).
  3. The dataset must include two types of tables:

   - Content Data: Includes content unique ID, content title, and content URL.

   - Learner Data: Includes content ID, user ID, date, etc.

Structure:

This document is divided into four parts:

  1. Login Method (SSO) for Accessing the LxP
  2. Sending User Data from SuccessFactors to the LxP
  3. Receiving Content Data from LxP into SuccessFactors
  4. Receiving Learning History from LxP to SuccessFactors

1. Login Method (SSO) for Accessing the LxP

Method 1: SuccessFactors Outbound SSO

You can connect the third-party LxP to SuccessFactors SSO similarly to other OCNs. Use SAML SSO (e.g., x256 encryption) to log in to the LxP.
- Advantage: Single login for users.
- Disadvantage: SuccessFactors only sends UserID and EmailID, so additional attributes need to be sent separately. Some LxPs may not support flat-file-based user creation.

Method 2: Identity Management Solutions (Recommended)

If your organization uses centralized identity management solutions like Azure AD, Okta, or ADFS, you can authenticate users for both SuccessFactors and the third-party LxP. The Identity Provider (IdP) should send the BizX UserID to both SuccessFactors and the LxP. Ensure that user IDs are identical between both systems to enable learner activity tracking.
- Advantages: Single login, standardized integration, no need for LMS-specific consultants.
- Disadvantage: If the IdP doesn’t provide the BizX UserID, integration may face challenges, but these can be mitigated in later steps.

Method 3: SAP Identity Management (IAS)

This method is similar to Method 2, where SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) manages user authentication across systems.

2. Sending User Data from SuccessFactors to the LxP

The LxP needs to create user profiles and send learner history back to LMS. Multiple options are available to send user data:

  1. Using Active Directory: Share available attributes from Active Directory to the LxP.
  2. Using Integration Center: Send user data via flat files.
  3. Using Post API: The LxP's API can receive user data directly.

Note: The LxP must receive the BizXID (UserID) from SuccessFactors. If this isn’t feasible, you can map email and user IDs via CPI during step 4.

3. Receiving Content Data (Item Data) from LxP to SuccessFactors

The only way to receive content data is through the item connector. This connector fetches content data from the LxP via API or flat file.

Item Connector Requirements:

- ItemID: Unique identifier for the content (required).

- Item Type: Optional, but helps distinguish content categories (can default).

- Item Title: Required.

- Creation Date: Optional (default values can be used).

- Content URL: Required.

- Other attributes, such as content description, are optional.

Best Practices:

- Keep common attributes (e.g., item type, revision date, revision ID) as defaults.

- If the content URL is static, only the item data connector is necessary.

- If the content URL is dynamic or multiple URLs exist for a single content item, you will need to create an additional connector file for item module data.

While the item connector can function with minimal data points, adding additional attributes can improve the overall integration.

4. Receiving Learning History Data from LxP to SuccessFactors

There are two methods for sending learning history data from the LxP to SuccessFactors LMS:

API Method

The preferred approach is to use the admin/learningevent-service/v1 API. This method allows real-time transmission of learning data from the LxP to SuccessFactors. CPI fetches the data from the LxP (via API or flat file), converts it to the LMS API standard, and sends it to LMS.

Learning History Connector

This alternative method involves the Learning History Connector. CPI collects learner history daily, sending it via SFTP on a scheduled basis. However, unlike the API method, this approach does not update records instantly.

 

 

By following these steps, you can successfully integrate a custom third-party LxP with SAP SuccessFactors LMS. This integration allows for seamless user access, content management, and learning history tracking across both platforms.

references:
2318341 - LMS Connectors Knowledge Support and Tips 
SAP SuccessFactors Learning OData APIs

entry.title: Re: Can we share the Alert notification service instance among different spaces from one subaccount
entry.content: i think it depends on the scope of the event, if event scope ans:eventScope is CF_SPACE, ans instance residing in other space of the subaccount can read events in other spaces.

entry.title: Re: Finding Tax Rate for Custom CDS View
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you check if the CDS View I_CAInvcgDocItem can fulfill your requirement?

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: SAP BTP ANS - Is there an attribute that can be used in ANS action to capture the subaccount name?
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP BTP ANS, Is there an attribute that can be used in ANS action to capture the subaccount name? 

resource.subAccount is capturing only the GUID.

entry.title: SAP BTP ANS - Is WSDL webservice supported in actions?
entry.content: Hello - I have a webservice using WSDL SOAP. Can i setup an action in SAP BTP Alert Notification System to call this webservice?

entry.title: Re: Output Management - Configurando o DANFE(Adobe Forms)
entry.content:
Boa noite João, beleza pura?

Rapaz, a nota SAP 3210908 incluiu na J1BNFE um botão de EXIBIR PDF.

Notei que esse botão está fazendo a chamada do serviço standard, ignorando o serviço Z que foi criado para ser chamado no botão de imprimir a DANFE. O botão de imprimir funciona 100%.
Voce saberia nos dizer se existe algum local para configurar qual serviço será chamado ao clicar no no botão EXIBIR PDF da J1BNFE?

entry.title: Recovery from deleted purchasing groups in SRM
entry.content:
We have a number of purchasing groups and positions that were deleted in SRM.  We tried reinstating records in HRP100, HRP1001, HRP5500 and PLOGI which let us select the old group in workload distribution to assign the shopping carts and purchase orders associated with the old group to the ones we recreated.  However, drafts and documents pending approval are not listed for reassignment there.  Those documents are short dumping and giving users the message below when users try to edit them.  What can we do to resolve this?

Error message to users:

 

Short dump:

ategory ABAP programming error
untime Errors OBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED_NO
xcept. CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL
BAP Program /SAPSRM/CL_PDO_META_BO_SC_ADV=CP
pplication Component SRM-EBP-SHP
ate and Time 10/14/2024 22:12:34

 

Short Text
Access using a 'NULL' object reference is not possible.

What happened?
Error in the ABAP application program.

The current ABAP program "/SAPSRM/CL_PDO_META_BO_SC_ADV=CP" had to be
terminated because it found a
statement that could not be executed.

Error analysis
An exception has occurred which is explained in more detail below. The
exception is assigned to class 'CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL' and was not caught in
procedure
"/SAPSRM/IF_PDO_META_CONSUMER~GET_SET_TYPE_METADATA" "(METHOD)", nor was it
propagated by a RAISING clause.

 

>>>>> CALL METHOD mo_meta_app_view->get_set_type_metadata
17 EXPORTING
18 iv_set_type = iv_set_type
19 iv_parent = iv_parent
20 RECEIVING
21 ev_exist = lv_exist.
22
23 IF lv_exist NE abap_undefined.
24 ev_exist = lv_exist.
25 ENDIF.

(IV_SET_TYPE = 39, IV_PARENT = 16)

entry.title: Business application studio: dev space stuck in status 'Starting'
entry.content:
Hi, 

My dev space stuck in status 'Starting' for a long time and I can't enter my dev space. Anyone has a solution to get it into the 'Running' status ?

The workspace ID: ws-vllvb

entry.title: How to rectify the decimal variations of GRN in sap
entry.content:
I have posted the GRN of PO qty 0.7 with 3 different GRNs qty of 0.02, 0.28, 0.4. 
Part price is 3763.77.
PO value is 2634.65.
Next we have posted the LIV, At that time we found the price difference of 2634.64.
Price diff. of 0.01
So Kindly share your valuable solution of this issue.

table MHIP

t-code: IP24

t-code: IP10

@S0022314014

entry.title: Edit button in business configuration app is disabled
entry.content:
Hi ,

I created business configuration for one of my table in the BTP environment using the following steps.

- https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-business-configuration-object.html

- https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-authorization-control.html

But somehow the edit button in the business configuration app is still disabled.

entry.title: Re: Push S/4 HANA Data Event to BTP Event Mesh in ABAP OO Mode
entry.content:
Hi Mikel,

For EM, you can use 'SAP NetWeaver AddOn for Event Enablement'. download and install it. any custom topic can be used via this approach.

for AEM, you need to look at 'SAP RAP Business Event' via SAP enterprise event enablement for custom topic.

So do we need to vectorize all newly created data and store it in Hana Cloud constantly, sot that it will be always up to date ?

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 Release Highlights : SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Updates to Watch Out For
entry.content:
Can you elaborate why we are not seeing Reports counters :

- Direct reports

- Team Size

in Spotlight view ? 

HR & IS processes have lost the ONE STOP / FIT ALL features that enabled multiples processes to identify wether a person is a Manager or an employee.

Now these requires to display Org chart ( and leave the current menu in SuccessFactors) while we use to be able to search in global search for an employee name and using the mouse over feature be able to check the number reports of these employee (and therefore knowing it was a manager or not)

These looks like a BIG GAP opened between B2405 & B2411 release.

i don't see any tiles enabled in spotlight, which have these information : 

- reporting lines tile have only upper level manager but don't display any information on direct reports

@DianaLiden do you have any informations to share on these ?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/solman-7-2-system-copy/qaq-p/12697895

Regards

SS

entry.title: SAP Fiori Custom Variant Migration: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
1. Prerequisites
    Exporting Custom Variants from the Source System

To migrate custom variants, you must first export them from the source system. Follow these steps:

Step 1: Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Log into your SAP Fiori Launchpad on the source system.
  • Navigate to the app where your custom variants are configured.

Step 2: Access the Custom Variant Management

  • Click on the filter icon or other settings icons where the variant settings are accessible.
  • Select the "Manage Variants" option.

     

Step 3: Export the Custom Variant

  • In the variant management dialog, locate the export option (this might be labeled differently based on the app or version).
  • Select the custom variant you want to export.
  • Click the "Export" button and save the file to your local system.

Note: The exported file will typically be in XML or JSON format, which will be used for importing on the target system.

  1. Importing Custom Variants to the Target System

Now that you have exported the custom variants, it's time to import them into the target system.

Step 1: Log into the Target System's Fiori Launchpad

  • Ensure you have access to the same app on the target system.
  • Open the Fiori Launchpad and navigate to the relevant app.

Step 2: Access the Import Functionality

  • Go to the variant management dialog, similar to the source system.
  • Locate the "Import" option.

Step 3: Upload the Exported File

  • Click the "Import" button and select the file you exported earlier.
  • Confirm the import, and the custom variant will be added to the target system.

Tip: Ensure there are no naming conflicts. If a variant with the same name already exists, consider renaming it before importing.

  1. Verify the Migration

Once the import is complete, verify that the custom variants have been correctly migrated. Follow these steps:

  • Access the app on the target system.
  • Check if the variants appear as expected.
  • Test the settings to confirm they match those from the source system.
  1. Troubleshooting Common Issues

Here are some common problems you might encounter during the migration process and how to resolve them:

Issue 1: Import/Export Button Not Visible

  • Ensure you have the correct authorization.
  • Check if variant management is enabled for that particular app.

Issue 2: Exported File Format Is Not Supported

  • Verify that both the source and target systems are compatible.
  • Check for SAP Notes or updates that address this issue.
  1. Conclusion

Migrating custom variants in SAP Fiori can save a lot of time and effort, especially when moving to a new environment or upgrading your systems. By following the steps outlined in this guide, you can ensure a smooth migration process.

Please comment on the issues not listed in this blog you have encountered during migration.
Cheers!
PS: Screen shots have been minimized in order to hide system data

entry.title: Re: Looking for guidance to separate Datasphere workspace.
entry.content: That totaly depends on your own Company and your data Strategy. You should have a look at Data Mesh and Hub&Spoke.

Request your help, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Applying a prepayment in the Enter incoming invoice a...
entry.content:
Hello YMoro, You may please refer to the scope item J60 for the complete prepayment process. It has the prepayment/down payment process in detail.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Updating the Value Categories for Maintenance Orders in S4HC Public Edition for Asset Management
entry.content: Hi, Do you have a 3 tier public cloud environment? If so, it should be relatively easy to just make the config change in your environment and test the behaviour? I would guess that it works the same as Private Cloud and the older maintenance orders arent updated automatically. You used to have to run that program you refer to to update the PRCO tables after the fact, so it should be that only new maintenance orders get the new values posted into the value categories you have modified. If you do test it out, please let us know how it goes. Tim

entry.title: Re: SAP Activate Project manager Certification

Your proposed solution is not solving my use case (list emplpoyees from PAxxxx Tables, Display Mode only - object page with CRUD for Documents for a selected Employee. Forget about all the my other headaches. Please read my last reply again. If you think your solution works, then please attach all artefacts and I will try it out on our system.

entry.title: Re: Applying a prepayment in the Enter incoming invoice a...
entry.content:
Hello Santosh,
I have checked the test script of J60.
The test script says that the Create Supplier Invoice App (ID: F0859) is used to create an accounts payable and the Clear Outgoing Payments App (ID: F1367) is used to apply the prepayment.
I want to know if the Enter incoming invoice app (ID: MIRO) can create an accounts payable and reconcile prepayments at the same time.

Thank you!

I'm from Jordan working in KSA using SAP/SUCCESSFACTOR system and would like to join a group that has some discussion about best practice use in the middle east.

Hope if someone can support

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 Release Highlights : Unlocking the Power of AI in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Does all these features require AI units? Are there any free AI features that we can use as a customer?

entry.title: Cost Center Planning in S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Could you please guide in this regard that when we do the cost center planning with Plan Category in FIORI via Import Financial Plan Data application without BW route, that time in FIORI we could able to see the plan data has been imported successfully. But when we are trying to see the plan data in report S_ALR_87013611 that time we are not able to see the plan data there. Even we are not able to see the plan data in ACDOCP table. 

So could you please guide in this regard. Your prompt response will be highly highly appreciated.

Regards,

Varun

entry.title: Re: cumulative amounts
entry.content: Hi Daniel, there are multiple ways to create cumulative amounts in SAC. Here is a blog focus on "cumulative sum" in SAC. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/everything-you-need-to-know-about-cumulative-sum-in-sap-analytics-cloud/ba-p/13569357

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

I’m Muhammad Talha Waseem, currently a SAP B1 Functional consultant in training at Hussain Ch & Co (HCC) with over two years of hands-on experience in SAP B1 ERP. As a CA finalist student from ICAP, my journey in integrating business processes with technology has been both rewarding and insightful. I'm passionate about leveraging SAP B1 to provide tailored solutions to clients in trading, manufacturing, and real estate sectors. Excited to connect, learn, and share knowledge with fellow professionals here!

entry.title: Re: cumulative amounts
entry.content: Hi Daniel, there are multiple ways to create cumulative amounts in SAC. Here is a blog focus on "cumulative sum" in SAC. please check the blog and see if it can help.https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/everything-you-need-to-know-about-cumulative-sum-in-sap-analytics-cloud/ba-p/13569357

entry.title: Error while making a HTTP request from ABAP REPORT PROGRAM
entry.content:
I am trying to make a post request to an extrnal URL to fetch the CSRF token details which are further required to make a get request. I have attached the code below. The code is working fine in the ECC system but it is giving the error in S/4 system. Is the error related to some system configuration?

Error Text:- IcmConnInitClientSSL: Proxy connection to https://internal.yamaha-dealers.com:443 via proxy:3128 failed

CODE:-

cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
    EXPORTING
      url    = 'https://internal.yamaha-dealers.com/oauth2/endpoint/YMUSProvider/token'
      proxy_host = 'proxy'
      proxy_service = '3128'
   IMPORTING
     client = ro_client
     ).
    ro_client->refresh_request( ).
    ro_client->request->set_version( if_http_request=>co_protocol_version_1_1 ).
    ro_client->propertytype_logon_popup = 0.

    "Set Method
    ro_client->request->set_method( 'POST').

    "Set headers
    ro_client->request->set_header_fields( it_header_fields ).

    DATA(lo_request) = cl_rest_message_builder=>create_http_message_entity( ro_client->request ).
    lo_request->set_content_type( iv_media_type = 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' ).
    lo_request->set_string_data( 'grant_type=password&client_id=sap_user&client_secret=$E#P7vb9pgGp&username=U:externalService:sap_user&password=22Yam?38' ).

    ro_client->request->set_header_field( name  = if_http_header_fields=>accept
                                          value = if_rest_media_type=>gc_appl_www_form_url_encoded ).
it_header_fields:-

Please let me know if any more info is required.

@SAPSupport @DominikTylczyn @Mrak1 @fabio_perfetto @Margit_Wagner @AndreasMuno @Dsk @Enda @Christian_Ries @tamas_hoznek @Sandra_Rossi

entry.title: Re: Use SAP Datasphere as a source for the SAP BW bridge
entry.content:
It is interesting to see that SAP uses the concept of ABAP CDS entities to consume data from SAP Datasphere models in BW Brdige and not the concept of datasources. @heiko_schneider In addition to views, can the external CDS entities also query data from local tables or analytic models and also access associated dimensions or hierarchies?

@Sebastian_Gesiarz : One use case would be, for example, if you need Non-SAP data in staging in the BW Bridge, e.g. lookups in transformations. Currently, you can only implement such a use case directly in Datasphere, but you may want to reuse existing logic from your BW system in the BW Bridge and not rebuild everything in Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: How to use filter in ui5 table
entry.content: In my case, I was using `filterProperty` value incorrectly. I was using `template>name` but correct value required was only `name`.

entry.title: Re: Customer Data Migration - From General to Sales and C...
entry.content:
Hi Salvio,

Consider using standard "data migration cockpit".

Kind regards,

Jack.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Banner/Background Image uploading access to employee

entry.title: Re: About the default condition types of item in Sales order
entry.content:
Hi Jason,

Click "Analysis" from your screenshot, you could get which pricing procedure you were using. Then go to SPRO background configuration to search previous pricing procedure which you already gotten, you could see many default condition types there.

Kind regards,

Jack.

entry.title: Re: Custom SAP BTP Workzone Maintaince mode
entry.content:
In CIS your user has an end-date which can be used to show the message up front by using the CIS API.

CIS end date:

With the CIS API, you can get this information and show a messages in Work Zone by using a plugin:

API docu: SP User Information | SAP Help Portal

Work Zone plugin: Add a Shell Plugin | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Trial from CAL
entry.content:
I wonder if the S/4 HANA provided by CAL supports it as a BRIM solution.
We are SAP's partner, and we need the test & demo version of S/4 HANA Private cloud or on-premise.
Or, if you know how to use the test & demo version of the Subscription Order management/Convergent Invoicing/FICA module, please let me know.

entry.title: IBP-Unable to match the count of "Timeseries data" with "Analyze Data Volume(ADV) in Calculation
entry.content:
Module: SAP Integrated Business Planning
Program: Analyze Data Volume(ADV) in Calculations

  1. I have created Planning area(PA) with KF attributes and calculations.
  2. I ran the Analyze Data Volume in Calculations for my PA
  3. Post sucessful run, I am trying to compare the data volumne reported in "Analyze Data Volume" calcuation post run with the "IBP Excel UI KF Data series" count.
    eg. my KF data count is around 31 and the ADV data is around 72. I think these numbers should match. please correct me if I am wrong.
  4. The reason I am trying to match is to see in the first place, the output to the ADV program should match the data input feed to ADV from the PA/Time series data created.
    This will help me analyse that the execution run is request on the correct count of data/base set of data , so that I can analyse further the performance/execution outcome timings.

entry.title: Is it ok to stop VMs when instances of SAP S4/HANA are not in use to build SAP S4/HANA?
entry.content:
I am considering building an environment for SAP S4/HANA on-prem version,
I am considering setting up a server on GCP to build the environment.
I would like to stop the VM when S4/HANA is not in use in order to reduce operation costs.
Is this a problem?

entry.title: adobe form still inactive
entry.content: SOAP Framework error: SOAP Runtime Exception: CSoapExceptionTransport : HTTP receive failed with exception communication_failure  (100,101).

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

My name is Falak and recently I’ve started exploring the world of data analytics, and that’s when I came across SAP. With my background in business administration, I’ve always been curious about how data can influence decision-making, and learning about SAP has really sparked my interest, especially in the areas of planning and financial management.

The wide range of tools and solutions SAP provides are really impressive, and I’m excited to learn more through the available courses. Getting SAP certified is something I’m working towards, as I believe it will boost my skills and match my passion for using data to improve strategic planning.

I’ve found that the SAP Digital Skills Initiative Program is the perfect way for me to get certified and move my career in the right direction. I’m really looking forward to this certification as the learning journeys have been valuable for me and hope to make a difference in the industry with the help of SAP’s powerful tools.

entry.title: Re: BOM的项目组件无法指定生产版本信息
entry.content:
你好 @omega-ou ,

物料清单（BOM）和生产版本的管理是两个相对独立的模块。你提到的问题涉及到如何在物料清单中指定组件的生产版本，这在标准的物料清单维护界面中确实是无法直接实现的。

以下是一些可能的解决方案和建议:

1. 在物料主数据中维护生产版本
确保在物料主数据（物料主记录）中正确维护了半成品B的生产版本信息。步骤如下：
- 进入物料主数据维护界面。
- 选择“生产版本”视图。
- 在该视图中维护不同的生产版本及其相关信息。

2. 使用替代物料
如果半成品B的不同生产版本在物料清单中的使用有明显的业务规则，可以考虑使用替代物料的方式来处理。通过维护替代物料和相应的替代策略，可以在生产过程中灵活选择不同的生产版本。

3. 使用生产版本管理生产订单
在SAP S/4HANA中，生产版本主要用于生产订单和计划订单的管理。你可以在生产订单或计划订单中指定使用哪个生产版本。具体步骤如下：
- 创建生产订单或计划订单时，系统会根据物料主数据中的生产版本策略自动选择合适的生产版本。
- 你也可以手动在生产订单或计划订单中选择生产版本。

4. 自定义开发
如果标准功能无法满足你的需求，可以考虑通过ABAP开发进行自定义。你可以与技术团队合作，在物料清单维护界面中添加生产版本字段，并在后台逻辑中处理相关的生产版本选择。

在标准的SAP S/4HANA Cloud中，物料清单维护界面确实没有直接的生产版本字段。你可以通过在生产订单或计划订单中指定生产版本，或者通过自定义开发来实现你的需求。如果业务需求较为复杂，建议使用高级计划和优化模块或集成业务计划来处理。

希望这些建议对你有所帮助。如果有进一步的问题，欢迎继续咨询。

祝好，

Dave He (何俊杰)

entry.title: Re: Search Functionality Not Working in SuccessFactors Extension Application

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process Manager - Attribute Visualizatio...
entry.content: Hi, your welcome. You should leave the field in the process blank. Then it should get the information out of the glossary.

entry.title: Re: Applying a prepayment in the Enter incoming invoice a...
entry.content: Hi, It is possible to configure the prepayment in the Logistics Invoice Verification in OP, however, there is not such function on public cloud. You'd better go for advance payment to vendor which is included in scope item J60, Or go for partial payment if invoice created. Kind Regards, Lucy

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Eleme...
entry.content:
Hi Patrick

Please see the screenshots attached. IMHO this is your solution 1:1 implemented according to your proposition (from your attachment). 

Mind you, my very first attempt was to solve the use case with a fake Emplyoee Table with internal create, update, delete in Behvour Definition. But then use a CDS Employee Root View (from PAxxxx Tables) according to my use case.


The employee list (from PAxxxx Tables) is crucial! This simple requirement is what makes the use of RAP, Fiori Elements and FPM impossible. Please prove me wrong!

Thanks Paul

entry.title: Re: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Eleme...
entry.content: Please see my updated answer with screeshots of your proposed solutiuon

entry.title: Re: Applying a prepayment in the Enter incoming invoice a...
entry.content:
Hello YMoro,

Creating/posting a downpayment with respect to a PO and clearing the DP on MIRO is not possible, but creating a DPR and posting the DP (without reference to PO) and later clearing against a supplier invoice is still possible.

Thank You!

entry.title: Dynamic file naming in TPM AS2 Receiver
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have got a requirement to incorporate message ID of Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow V2 as a dynamic filename in AS2 Receiver channel of TPM.

Can anyone provide me the solution on how the message ID of the particular iflow can be called as camel header and how the same can be set in TPM as AS2 receiver dynamic filename.

 

Thanks,

Aditya

Has anybody else upgraded to this PL?

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 Release Highlights : SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Updates to Watch Out For
entry.content: @nageshpolu Thank you, this blog is informative. Such documentation helps facilitate the adoption of new features and maintains the trust between SAP and its customers

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start V2.0 - Release Update
entry.content:
With the latest release of SAP Mobile Start, you get significant enhancements that will make your usage of SAP Mobile Start even more enjoyable and efficient. Besides Spaces & Pages and declarative integration cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, you will also get some nice enhancements for tasks. 

Spaces & – New Experience
Introduced for the web some time back, we now support Spaces & Pages also in SAP Mobile Start. This comes with several advantages for you, like an improved organization and categorization of your content - as you do know already from the web. You get a more structured view of your applications and information, making it easier to locate and access your relevant resources quickly. 
If you use groups to structure your apps, your groups will also be shown on sub-pages on the Apps screen in the future, what significantly improves user experience.

Screenshot: Spaces & Pages

Cards
So far, SAP Mobile Start has supported insight cards from SAP Start. With the release 2.0, we do support the “New Experience” of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and you will get your declarative integration cards, embedded in the respective pages on the Apps screen. By tapping on the card, you navigate into the card details. Depending on the functional scope of a card, actions can be directly triggered from it. If you do use cards in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition already, then they will automatically appear for you within the SAP Mobile Start version 2.0.

 

Screenshot: Cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

SAP S/4HANA Situations Handling
Situation Handling informs you about situations that need your attention. This includes for example upcoming deadlines, delays, expiring contracts, and so on. With SAP Mobile Start version 2.0, we now also support the standard framework capabilities of Situation Handling. You always find your latest situations in your Latest To-Dos section on the Start screen and all situations on the To-Dos screen.

Tasks
Tasks are one key element of SAP Mobile Start as they ensure that you are always on top of your relevant business workflows. Task-based notifications and widgets help to ensure that you never oversee or forget an important task. With the latest version of SAP Mobile Start, you can see URL attachments for tasks in the new links section within the attachments on the task details screen. By tapping on the link, the respective link is opened in a new window. Typical links are a link to a document stored on a file server or a link to an ISO document.

With version 2.0, the app also supports non-finalising actions for tasks. Those are mainly relevant for tasks that come from partner solutions. With a non-finalizing task action you can update the task without complete it. Typical non-finalising actions are to increase a task priority or change the task status.

 Screenshot: Non-finalising actions for tasks

Finally, you get a new To-Dos detail page layout for Android! It was specifically designed for larger screens like tablets and foldable devices. With the booming market for foldables and tablets on Android, there's a growing need to optimize our layouts for larger screens.

Screenshot: Task details large screen on Android 

I hope you will enjoy those new features in SAP Mobile Start.

For further information on the new topics, please check our SAP Mobile Start documentation.

SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: CAP LLM Plugin vs GenAI javascript SDK + document grounding

Venkatesh

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve data from a fieldymbol nested of deep...
entry.content: Hi Juan, thank you very much for your hint. I will try whether this might help and keep you up to date.

entry.title: Re: Access Jdbc Material while developing a Custom Adapte...
entry.content:
It is incredibly easy if you know SAP CI uses OSGi:

javax.sql.DataSource ds = new InitialContext().lookup(
    "osgi:service/javax.sql.DataSource/(osgi.jndi.service.name=YOUR-NAME)",
) as javax.sql.DataSource

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Communication in Recruitment: A Deep Dive into SuccessFactors Message Center
entry.content: @SachinPachauri Thank you, this blog is informative. Such documentation helps facilitate the adoption of new features and maintains the trust between SAP and its customers.

entry.title: 5 Major Generative AI Innovations Unveiled at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
At SAP TechEd 2024, SAP revealed several key innovations centered around Generative AI, further enhancing its ecosystem and offering businesses powerful tools to drive digital transformation. These new capabilities focus on simplifying complex processes, boosting customization, and integrating AI into the core of enterprise applications. Here are the five major Generative AI announcements that will shape the future of SAP’s intelligent enterprise solutions.

1. Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud in SAP Build

One of the standout announcements is the introduction of the Generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud, embedded within SAP Build. This new toolkit allows users to execute data analysis and perform advanced machine learning tasks effortlessly.

By using the AI-powered capabilities available through Joule—SAP’s intelligent assistant—users can now complete complex tasks like generating time-series forecast models using natural language prompts. This eliminates the need for deep technical knowledge in machine learning, making AI accessible to business users and enabling faster, data-driven decision-making.

2. AI-Driven Optimization in SAP Integration Suite

SAP’s Integration Suite now comes equipped with Generative AI-driven optimization for custom scripts. This feature allows users to open the script editor and automatically receive AI-generated recommendations to optimize their code.

This enhancement empowers developers and business users alike by reducing manual effort and improving efficiency. The AI-driven optimizations make integration tasks smoother and more reliable, enhancing the overall performance of custom solutions.

3. Joule Studio: Design Custom AI Skills with Low-Code Tools

Joule Studio, a new feature within SAP Build, offers a low-code platform for creating custom AI skills that integrate seamlessly with your SAP landscape. With Joule Studio, developers can build intelligent applications without extensive coding knowledge, using a simplified interface.

For instance, if you need to bring ServiceNow tickets into Joule, you can easily create a custom skill using Joule Studio. The platform makes it simple to configure API parameters, deploy the skill, and instantly retrieve data from within Joule, making it an incredibly efficient solution for businesses looking to streamline their workflows and automate tasks.

Joule Studio will enter beta later this year, with general availability expected shortly after.

4. Enhanced Generative AI Hub: New Models and Capabilities

SAP also introduced a major upgrade to the Generative AI Hub, a platform that integrates state-of-the-art AI models with SAP applications. The hub now supports closed and open-source models from leading providers like Meta and Mistral, enabling businesses to harness the best of both foundational models and real-time data from SAP systems.

Key enhancements include:
- Orchestration services for managing AI workflows.
- Data masking and content filtering capabilities for improved privacy and security.
- New prompt templates and prompt management tools, making it easier to deploy AI models and create custom solutions.

Developers will also have access to new SDKs for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, ensuring a seamless integration of AI into a variety of SAP environments.

5. SAP Knowledge Graph: Unlocking AI-Powered Insights

SAP’s Knowledge Graph, combined with the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, is set to revolutionize how businesses interact with SAP data. The Knowledge Graph provides a vast repository of structured metadata, including over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views, and more than 7 million properties and fields.

By linking Large Language Models (LLMs) with the semantic model of SAP, the Knowledge Graph enables powerful use cases, such as:
- AI-driven predictions and insights.
- Advanced information retrieval capabilities.
- Enhanced analytics across the SAP landscape.

This comprehensive dataset serves as a bridge between natural language processing (NLP) and SAP’s structured data, opening up new opportunities for AI-driven innovation. The SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine will be available in Q1 2025, offering developers a robust foundation for building advanced business applications.

Why These Announcements Matter

SAP’s focus on Generative AI at TechEd 2024 highlights a clear shift toward making AI more accessible to all users—whether they’re business professionals, developers, or data scientists. These innovations are designed to reduce the complexity of working with AI while enhancing its ability to solve real-world business problems.

With the introduction of tools like the Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud and Joule Studio, users can now build intelligent, AI-driven applications with ease. The Generative AI Hub and SAP Knowledge Graph further empower developers to leverage cutting-edge models and tap into SAP’s vast structured data for advanced analytics and business insights.

As these tools become available, businesses can expect a significant boost in their ability to automate processes, gain actionable insights, and create smarter, more efficient solutions in the SAP ecosystem.

Stay tuned for the full release of these features in the coming months, as SAP continues to lead the charge in bringing AI to enterprise systems in powerful and accessible ways.

entry.title: Post processing framework (PPF) on ECC6 EHP8 issues
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to use the standard PPF functionality hence started working on a custom action profile using a persistent class. Idea is to call a ppf action by passing some parameters as application object in my persistent class. Here is my configuration.

There are no schedule or start conditions.

My issue is that after the method determine( ) is called from ppf manager, the badi processing do not get the application object reference. The context information is correctly passed during determination via ppf manager but still in the processing method, the object IO_APPL_OBJECT is empty. 

Here are some screenshots from debugging.

  1. Initiating the action from a test program
  2. In the BADI implementation, the application object is empty.

     

Let me know what I am missing here. Thanks in advance.
ABAP Development  #PPF 
@qmacro @thomas_jung 

~Gurpreet

entry.title: Data Discovery SAC Live Connection
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

I am requesting your help. I am currently trying to read information from a table in a live connection model from PaPM. We have a script that, upon page initialization, can read the information directly from the table when it was created from a CSV file data source. This table can be read perfectly through the Analytics Designer Table API.

I've been researching, and it seems that live connection does not support data discovery. Is there any way to achieve this? I am sharing the script below of what I've got so far.

 

 

var dataSource = Table_1.getDataSource();
var dimensions = dataSource.getDimensions(); // Obtiene las dimensiones
var measures = dataSource.getMembers("Measures", 1000); // Obtiene los miembros de la dimensión "Measures" (medidas)


var accData = dataSource.getResultSet();


var stringCSV = "";


var i = 0;
var j = 0;
var k = 0;
var m = 0;
var n = 0;
var tempString = ""; // Inicializada como cadena vacía
var dimId = ""; // Inicializada como cadena vacía
var measureName = ""; // Inicializada como cadena vacía
var dimensionValue = ""; // Para valores de dimensión
var measureValue = ""; // Para valores de medida


for (i = 0; i < dimensions.length; i++) {
tempString = stringCSV + dimensions[i].description + ",";
stringCSV = tempString;
}


for (j = 0; j < measures.length; j++) {
if (j < measures.length - 1) {
tempString = stringCSV + measures[j].description + ","; // Usa 'description' para nombre legible de la medida
} else {
tempString = stringCSV + measures[j].description + "\n";
}
stringCSV = tempString;
}


for (k = 0; k < accData.length; k++) {
// Recorre las dimensiones dinámicamente
for (m = 0; m < dimensions.length; m++) {
dimId = dimensions[m].id; // Obtiene el ID de la dimensión


dimensionValue = accData[k][dimId].formattedValue || accData[k][dimId].id || "N/A";

tempString = stringCSV + dimensionValue + ",";
stringCSV = tempString;
}


for (n = 0; n < measures.length; n++) {
measureName = measures[n].id;


measureValue = accData[k][measureName].rawValue || "N/A";

if (n < measures.length - 1) {
tempString = stringCSV + measureValue + ",";
} else {
tempString = stringCSV + measureValue + "\n";
}
stringCSV = tempString;
}
}


contextData = stringCSV;

entry.title: Re: Notification in Financial Closing Cockpit
entry.content:
Hello, A similar discussion is already available. This may be helpful for you!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Warsaw 2024 – Key Highlights, Insights, and Community Feedback
entry.content:
Hi @sveabecker,

Thank you very much. Actually, there were slightly fewer people. The agenda was also less packed. This really helped with networking. We had plenty of time for conversations. Sometimes it was difficult to end the breaks and encourage participants to return to the room for the next presentation 😉 Even though the presentations were also very interesting!

entry.title: Ariba Integration with Non-SAP ERP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to integrate a Non-SAP ERP Procurement System with Ariba Business network for Commerce Automation and Implement Ariba Invoice Management to a Non-SAP ERP Finance System. Has anyone worked on this kind of integration? if so, below are some questions that i am looking for based on your experience in dealing with Ariba integration with Non-SAP ERP Systems. There is no option of Using BTP here.

1. Will Managed Gateway for Spend & Business Network (CIG) be in scope for this kind of integration with Non-SAP ERP Systems? If we are able to convert the Purchase order in the format required for Ariba Business network, can we use CIG as a pass-through to Network? If CIG is Out of Scope, are you aware that from external systems we can directly integrate Purchase orders to network?

2. For the Invoice Integration, When the invoice comes from Network to Ariba Invoice Mangement Solution (We dont have complete B&I, its only the Invoice Management solution in Ariba), will we be able to see the PO information i,e can we click on the PO and look for the PO details and will Invoice Management solution in this case can do the 2-way match and raise exceptions for differences?

3. With Ariba Invoice Management solution alone, do we get OCR scanning capabilities in-built? Has anyone worked on integrating Ariba Invoice Mangement with Non-SAP ERP Finance systems? If so, how was your integration model with and without CIG (If CIG is in scope for this kind of Non-SAP integrations?)

 

Any help that you can provide on the above questions would be much appreciated. 

Thanks a lot in advance!

Thanks,
Velu

#AribaInvoiceManagement #CommerceAutomation #AribaBusinessNetworkwithNon-SAPERP #IntegrationwithNon-SAP #CIGintegration with Non-SAP

entry.title: Re: SAC chart variance - "The time dimension does not hav...
entry.content: Hello @alberto_rosado, unfortunately no, the issue is not solved yet, so we have to go through workarounds. It's a pity it doesn't work as it should.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I identify myself as follows:

  • Women
  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location
  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 

 That being said, I am eager to take upon the new challenges and dive into the world of SAP. 

Regards.

entry.title: Enjoying SAP TechEd Virtual at the SAP Hamburg Location!
SAP Sybase Update
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I have a question about an update of ASE database, i recently made a mistake while updating were instead of using running the update through saphostctrl i used setup.bin from the extracted files. As a result i only updated the ASE but the backupserver is still on latest version so i am having trouble with bakcups now...

My question is, if i now just run the patch (hotfix in this case) using saphostctrl will it update bot backup server and database or do i need a roll back and execute saphostctrl from the previous version?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP CDS Distinct does not return distinct values
entry.content: Hello @catano,

entry.title: Re: Where is the field SBGRP(transaction FD33) in transac...
entry.content:
Hi,

SBGRP is not supported in FSCM. In transaction UKM_BP you can use Relationships. See KBA ##3028011 for further info:
KNKK-SBGRP (Credit representative group for credit management)
Should be maintained under relationships with type TUKMSBG. It would be saved in BUT050 table.
Also, for different selection purposes on Credit Profile you find Customer Credit Group option, for which you can define own groups as well.

Hope this helps.
Regards,
Emöke
SAP Support

entry.title: Try it out now : SAP Dynamic Forms generally available now in SAP BTP Trail Account
entry.content:
SAP Dynamic Forms - Trial edition is a trial subscription to the SAP Dynamic Forms BTP Application, an all-in-one solution that allows users to create, manage, and use digital forms that change in real-time as they are being filled out. Designers will be guided through the steps required to create a data-driven and responsive form, and users will be guided through the steps required to complete forms based on these designs.

Documentation Link : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DYNAMIC_FORMS?locale=en-US

SAP Dynamic forms Feature Scope : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DYNAMIC_FORMS/f9acfd39418a47cab622574ea9acaf84/7f9667d3844b46e6a3a9e17989dc675c.html?locale=en-US

SAP BTP Trial Account : https://cockpit.hanatrial.ondemand.com/trial/#

References :: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-dynamic-forms-1-0-released-features/ba-p/13748122

entry.title: Unable to approve PR/PO through email in S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
How to enable approve/reject button in email notification?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to approve PR/PO through email in S4 HANA publ...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer to the blog in SAP Community https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/how-to-set-up-the-email-notification-for-flexible-workflow-in-sap-s-4hana/bc-p/13524517

We can customise the email subject and the body. We also added the URL to navigate to the work item to My Inbox app. The receiver will click on the link that will open the related work item directly in the 'My Inbox' app 

However direct approver/rejection button in outlook functionality is not available.

SAP offers an opportunity for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers to submit improvement requests using this link: Homepage - Customer Influence (sap.com). Customer Influence (CIS) is a platform where customers can suggest features that they would like to see in future SAP HANA releases.

Please refer to the SAP KBA 0002963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product for more details.

Follow the below steps to open an Influence request:

  1. Get started by opening SAP Customer Influence.
  2. Register and accept the Terms of Use.
  3. Choose a session of your interest.
  4. Create and submit your improvement request.
  5. Take a look at existing improvement requests and start voting and commenting.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Script...
entry.content: Hello Mohammed, thank you for your detailed answer. However, I have the impression that you are trying to simplify tasks with GUI scripting that are not necessary at all when using PCA or others. Example: your script for SCC4. For what reason? PCA and other tools export the SCC4 component (tables T000 and CLMS_TENANT) and then import it - with the effect that SCC4 looks exactly the same as before. So what is the benefit of your script? Another example: your script for RZ04. PCA and other tools export the OPMODE component (tables BTCOMSET, TPFBA, TPFID) and then import it, with the effect that RZ04 looks exactly the same as before - there is no need to execute this transaction or do anything manually there. For me, the question is on what basis your article is based: for example, how are the users, the ALE configuration, the jobs, the SAP Office configuration and the RFC connections backed up and restored in your scenario? Or are your scripts only intended for checking / viewing the system status after the system refresh? Kind regards Jan
